methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Nimble Robotics is a Series C-stage robotics and manufacturing company currently valued at $1.0B post-money. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of eight private peers at same or adjacent stage (Series B–D) plus one public comparable, with peer valuations ranging from $300M to $10.0B.

Two methods triangulate the valuation. The stage-progression method applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the current $1.0B post-money, yielding $1.7B. The peer-median approach across eight same-sector comparables—including Hadrian, Exotec, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, and 1X Technologies—produces a $1.6B implied valuation. The wide peer range ($300M–$10.0B) reflects heterogeneity in robotics maturity and application, but the median and stage-progression results cluster tightly.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.4B (low) to $2.0B (high), with a median of $1.7B. This implies modest upside from the current $1.0B valuation under continued execution. Key uncertainties include the comp set's stage dispersion—peers span Series B through Series D—and the substantial outlier of 1X Technologies at $10.0B, which may reflect differentiated technology or market positioning. The analysis would benefit from greater granularity on revenue multiples or capital efficiency across the peer set to calibrate stage-appropriate expectations.
